title: podman pod stats failing to run on Ubuntu 20.04.1 on WSL2
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Running `podman pod stats POD_ID_HERE` returns the error `Error: pod stats is not supported in rootless mode without cgroups v2` but to my understanding cgroups v2 is already enabled for my installation of Ubuntu.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install podman

2. Play a K8s Pod or Deployment YAML file

3. Run `podman pod stats POD_ID_HERE`

**Describe the results you received:**

`Error: pod stats is not supported in rootless mode without cgroups v2`

**Describe the results you expected:**

Pod stats

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I am running Ubuntu 20.04.1 on WSL2 and to my understanding cgroups v2 is enabled.

```
$ grep cgroup /proc/filesystems
nodev   cgroup
nodev   cgroup2
```

The command `sudo mount -t cgroup2 none /data` runs just fine.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.1.0
API Version:  2.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Wed Dec 31 17:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

```

  cpus: 8

  hostname: DESKTOP-6LCTTUI
  idMappings:

  kernel: 4.19.104-microsoft-standard

  ociRuntime:
    name: crun

    version: |-
      crun version UNKNOWN
      commit: 3e46dd849fdf6bfa68127786e073318184641f05
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /tmp/run-1000/podman/podman.sock
  rootless: true
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns

    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.1.4
      commit: unknown
      libslirp: 4.3.1-git
      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3
  swapFree: 7516192768
  swapTotal: 7516192768
  uptime: 106h 4m 54.27s (Approximately 4.42 days)
registries:
  search:
  - docker.io
  - quay.io
store:
  configFile: /home/dakota/.config/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 8
    paused: 0

    number: 4
  runRoot: /tmp/run-1000/containers
  volumePath: /home/dakota/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes

  APIVersion: 2.0.0

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
$ apt list podman
Listing... Done
podman/unknown,now 2.1.0~1 amd64 [installed]
podman/unknown 2.1.0~1 arm64
podman/unknown 2.1.0~1 armhf
podman/unknown 2.1.0~1 s390x
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes, I am on the latest version as of writing this issue and I don't think I saw anything applicable to this error in the troubleshooting guide.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Windows 10 2004 OS Build 19041.508

```
PS > wsl --list --all --verbose
  NAME                   STATE             VERSION
* Ubuntu                 Running         2
```

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out!

`podman info` claims that you're running cgroups v1 so I fear there's not much we can do.
You may be running "hybrid mode" in which cgroups v1 and v2 are both available. Podman only supports native cgroups v2, however.

WSL2 Require cgroup v1 to be enabled, so we can't disable it. sad. 
https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/6662
Same problem with Podman on WSL

Same problem with Podman on WSL. 
I turned off the hybrid mode, tried various methods and tips that are available on the Internet for this request, nothing helps. The surprising thing is that the problem has been around since 2020
[wsl2]
kernelCommandLine=systemd.unified_cgroup_hierarchy=1 cgroup_no_v1=all
The problem is urgent, there is no solution for me

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md 
Item 16. I understand that in this this solution will only work fedora does not support cgroup v2
And then how to change the distribution to another one in WSL? fedora by default
